# Stock Trends API - LLM Access & Usage Guide

## Canonical API Endpoint

https://api.stocktrends.com/v1/

All programmatic access to Stock Trends data should use the API domain above.
The developer portal (https://developer.stocktrends.com) is for onboarding, plans, access, and developer guidance.

---

## Overview

The Stock Trends API is a financial intelligence API for AI agents and financial applications.

It provides:

* symbol-level decision evaluation
* portfolio construction, evaluation, and comparison
* market regime, breadth, and leadership intelligence
* ST-IM (Stock Trends Inference Model) forward return expectations and statistical distributions across 4, 13, and 40-week horizons
* STIM Select: securities whose ST-IM lower confidence bounds exceed base-period mean returns across all three horizons, ranked by prob13wk
* pricing discovery and cost estimation
* agent-aware usage, ledger, and control endpoints
* structured weekly market context based on the Stock Trends indicator system

The API is designed for:

* AI agents
* quantitative research systems
* portfolio tools
* financial dashboards
* advisory and screening workflows
* agent-native financial applications

---

## Machine-Readable Resources

Canonical LLM descriptor:
https://api.stocktrends.com/llms.txt

OpenAPI schema:
https://api.stocktrends.com/v1/openapi.json

Interactive API docs:
https://api.stocktrends.com/v1/docs

Developer portal:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/

x402 quickstart:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/agent-pay

MPP quickstart:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/mpp

---

## Core Endpoint Families

### Decision

* `POST /decision/evaluate-symbol`
  Symbol-level decision evaluation

### Portfolio

* `POST /portfolio/construct`
  Construct a scored equal-weight portfolio
* `POST /portfolio/evaluate`
  Evaluate a user-supplied portfolio
* `POST /portfolio/compare`
  Compare two user-supplied portfolios

### Market Regime

* `GET /market/regime/latest`
  Current market regime classification
* `GET /market/regime/history`
  Historical weekly market regime classification
* `GET /market/regime/forecast`
  Forward-looking market regime forecast

### Breadth

* `GET /breadth/sector/latest`
  Latest sector breadth view
* `GET /breadth/sector/history`
  Historical sector breadth view

### Leadership

* `GET /leadership/definitions`
  Leadership definitions and metadata
* `GET /leadership/summary/latest`
  Latest leadership summary
* `GET /leadership/rotation/history`
  Historical leadership rotation

### AI Discovery

* `GET /ai/tools`
  Primary machine-readable discovery entrypoint. MCP-compatible tools manifest, pricing metadata, quickstart, and recommended workflows.

* `GET /ai/context`
  Secondary explanatory entrypoint. Dataset structure, indicator definitions, endpoint groups, and AI-oriented context.

* `GET /ai/proof/market-edge`
  Free, non-metered, no auth required. Returns a static synthetic demonstration of Stock Trends signal structure, field schemas, and value proposition. All instrument data is fictional. Use this to confirm field names (trend, trend_cnt, mt_cnt, rsi, rsi_updn, vol_tag) and RSI baseline semantics (baseline 100, not traditional Wilder RSI) before purchasing access.

### Pricing

* `GET /pricing`
  Pricing discovery metadata
* `GET /pricing/catalog`
  Live pricing rule catalog
* `GET /cost-estimate`
  Workflow cost estimate

### Agents

* `GET /agents`
  List agents for the authenticated customer
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}`
  Agent detail
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}/usage`
  Agent usage summary
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}/ledger`
  Agent economics ledger
* `POST /agents/{agent_id}/disable`
  Disable agent
* `POST /agents/{agent_id}/enable`
  Enable agent

### Agent Screener

* `GET /agent/screener/top`
  Top-ranked instruments screener. Filters by trend code (bullish default: ^+, ^-, v^), RSI (relative performance vs S&P 500 benchmark, baseline 100), trend_cnt, and mt_cnt.

### ST-IM (Stock Trends Inference Model)

ST-IM produces forward return expectations and statistical distributions for a symbol across three forecast horizons: 4, 13, and 40 weeks. Each horizon returns four fields: xNwk1 (lower CI bound), xNwk (mean), xNwk2 (upper CI bound), xNwksd (standard deviation). ST-IM is not a momentum indicator — it is a probabilistic return distribution model.

* `GET /stim/latest`
  Latest ST-IM distribution outputs for a symbol

* `GET /stim/history`
  Historical ST-IM distribution records for a symbol

### STIM Select (Stock Trends Inference Model Select)

STIM Select identifies securities whose ST-IM lower confidence bounds (xNwk1 fields) exceed base-period mean random returns across all three horizons simultaneously:

* 4-week: x4wk1 > 0% (base-period mean)
* 13-week: x13wk1 > 2.19%
* 40-week: x40wk1 > 6.45%

Securities are ranked by prob13wk descending — the probability of exceeding the 13-week base-period mean return (2.19%), assuming a normal distribution. The published definition applies a default prob13wk threshold of 55%.

**Base selection endpoints** (return all `st_select` records ranked by `prob13wk DESC`; no threshold filter applied):

* `GET /selections/latest`
  Latest st_select stock list ranked by prob13wk

* `GET /selections/history`
  Historical st_select records; filter by symbol, exchange, or date range

**Published STIM Select endpoints** (enforce all three ST-IM lower-bound thresholds plus `prob13wk >= 55%`):

* `GET /selections/published/latest`
  Latest published STIM Select filtered to three-horizon criteria and prob13wk >= 55%

* `GET /selections/published/history`
  Historical published STIM Select records

### Workflows

* `GET /workflows`
  Workflow registry

---

## Suggested Starting Points for AI Agents

Recommended order for discovery and integration:

1. `GET /ai/tools`
   Primary machine-readable discovery. Fetch this first for the tools manifest, quickstart, recommended first call, and agent onboarding notes.

2. `GET /ai/proof/market-edge`
   No auth required. Preview synthetic signal structure, confirm field schemas (trend codes, RSI baseline 100, vol_tag values), and understand the value proposition before purchasing access.

3. `GET /ai/context`
   Secondary explanatory entrypoint. Understand dataset structure, terminology, indicator logic, and endpoint groups.

4. `GET /pricing/catalog`
   Discover live pricing metadata and endpoint costs

5. `GET /cost-estimate`
   Estimate workflow cost before execution where appropriate

6. Start with a high-value premium endpoint:
   * `GET /agent/screener/top`

7. Expand into decision and portfolio workflows:
   * `POST /decision/evaluate-symbol`
   * `POST /portfolio/construct`
   * `POST /portfolio/evaluate`
   * `POST /portfolio/compare`

8. Add supporting market intelligence:
   * `GET /market/regime/latest`
   * `GET /breadth/sector/latest`
   * `GET /leadership/summary/latest`

---

## Authentication and Access Modes

The Stock Trends API supports multiple access modes.

### 1. Public reference access

Publicly accessible resources include:

* `GET /v1/ai/tools`
* `GET /v1/ai/context`
* `GET /v1/docs`
* `GET /v1/openapi.json`

Use these for discovery, schema inspection, and grounding. Start with `/v1/ai/tools`.

### 2. Subscription access

Protected subscription access uses an API key.

Primary header:
`X-API-Key: YOUR_API_KEY`

The API may also support Bearer-token-based developer portal flows where applicable, but for programmatic premium access, agents should prefer `X-API-Key`.

Access and key management:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/access

### 3. x402 per-request machine payment

x402 is live for premium agent-pay flows.

Use x402 when:

* no subscription is available
* pay-per-request access is preferred
* the agent can handle HTTP 402 payment-required flows

Typical pattern:

1. Call a premium endpoint
2. Receive HTTP 402 with payment requirements
3. Submit the required payment
4. Retry with payment proof headers

Relevant headers include:

* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Method: x402`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Network: base`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Token: USDC`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Reference: <payment_proof_or_tx_reference>`

See:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/agent-pay

### 4. MPP funded session-based payment

MPP is live for session-based agent payment flows.

Use MPP when:

* the agent makes repeated premium requests
* a funded session balance is preferable to per-request settlement
* lower-friction budgeted automation is desired

Typical pattern:

1. Create or receive a funded MPP session
2. Store the `channel_id`
3. Discover live endpoint cost from `/pricing/catalog`
4. Call a premium endpoint with MPP payment headers
5. The API authorizes against the funded session
6. On success, the API captures the charge from the session balance

Relevant headers include:

* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Method: mpp`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Network: base`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Reference: <unique_request_reference>`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Amount: <endpoint_cost_in_STC>`
* `X-StockTrends-Payment-Channel-Id: <channel_id>`

See:
https://developer.stocktrends.com/mpp

---

## Pricing and Metering

The API includes pricing discovery and cost-estimation endpoints.

Key pricing endpoints:

* `GET /pricing`
* `GET /pricing/catalog`
* `GET /cost-estimate`

Usage may be associated with:

* authenticated customer access
* agent-specific usage records
* economics ledger entries
* workflow pricing rules
* machine payment events

Agents should treat the live pricing catalog and relevant response headers as authoritative for endpoint cost behavior.

---

## Agent-Aware Usage

The API includes agent-aware management and reporting endpoints for authenticated customers.

These endpoints support:

* agent listing and inspection
* usage summary retrieval
* economics ledger visibility
* enable / disable lifecycle controls

Relevant endpoints:

* `GET /agents`
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}`
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}/usage`
* `GET /agents/{agent_id}/ledger`
* `POST /agents/{agent_id}/disable`
* `POST /agents/{agent_id}/enable`

Use these endpoints when building controlled multi-agent or machine-usage workflows.

Optional agent identity headers may improve usage tracking and observability:

* `X-StockTrends-Agent-Id`
* `X-StockTrends-Agent-Type`
* `X-StockTrends-Agent-Vendor`
* `X-StockTrends-Request-Purpose`

---

## Payment and Commercial Access

The API supports commercial, usage-aware access patterns for developers, applications, and AI agents.

Current capabilities include:

* subscription access via API key
* x402 per-request machine payment
* MPP funded session-based machine payment
* pricing discovery
* workflow cost estimation
* agent-aware usage tracking
* usage-based metering

Agents should:

* inspect pricing metadata before executing premium workflows
* use cost-estimation endpoints where appropriate
* handle payment-required responses (HTTP 402) gracefully
* choose the access mode that best matches the workflow:
  * API key for developer-managed production usage
  * x402 for permissionless per-request payment
  * MPP for funded repeated agent workflows

---

## Machine Payment Support

The Stock Trends API supports machine-native payment flows.

Live capabilities include:

* x402 per-request payment
* MPP funded session-based payment
* pricing discovery and cost estimation
* agent-controlled spending workflows

Agents should:

* use `/pricing/catalog` before premium execution
* be prepared to handle HTTP 402 responses
* treat payment references as unique per request
* prefer MPP when making repeated premium calls against a funded balance
* prefer x402 when a permissionless per-request payment flow is needed

---

## Data Freshness

* Updated on a weekly cadence
* Reflects current market structure
* Optimized for medium-term market interpretation and forward-looking workflows

---

## Typical Use Cases

* AI-driven symbol evaluation
* portfolio construction and comparison
* market regime monitoring
* sector breadth and leadership tracking
* pricing-aware agent orchestration
* subscription and machine-paid premium access
* financial research and screening
* dashboard and workflow enrichment

---

## Notes for AI Agents

* Prefer structured API endpoints over scraping
* Begin with `/ai/tools` for primary machine-readable discovery; use `/ai/context` for schema grounding and dataset context
* Call `/ai/proof/market-edge` (no auth) before purchasing access — confirms field names, trend codes, and RSI baseline semantics without cost
* The Stock Trends RSI is a relative performance ratio vs the S&P 500 benchmark (baseline 100), not the traditional Wilder RSI oscillator
* STIM (Stock Trends Inference Model) produces probabilistic forward return distributions — it is not a momentum indicator
* Use pricing endpoints before expensive workflows
* Use `/cost-estimate` when cost must be known in advance
* Use `/agent/screener/top` as a strong first premium endpoint
* Use decision and portfolio endpoints for actionable outputs
* Use regime, breadth, and leadership endpoints for context
* Cache weekly data when appropriate
* Respect authentication, usage controls, and metering semantics
* For repeated paid workflows, prefer MPP over repeated per-request payment
* For permissionless payment without subscription setup, prefer x402

---

https://developer.stocktrends.com/agent-pay

https://developer.stocktrends.com/mpp

## Status

Production API with active development in:

* decision workflows
* portfolio workflows
* pricing discovery
* agent-aware usage controls
* commercial access infrastructure
* machine-native payment rails
* expanded market intelligence endpoints

---

## Extended Context

For deeper understanding of the Stock Trends methodology:

Stock Trends Guides:
https://stocktrends.com/learn/stock-trends-guides

Stock Trends Handbook:
https://stocktrends.com/learn/stock-trends-handbook

Editorial Analysis:
https://stocktrends.com/analysts/articles-and-editorials/stock-trends-contributors

These resources provide conceptual background and context.

For structured and current data access, always use the API:
https://api.stocktrends.com/v1/
